mnemonics.title: Try-Catch-Finally Flow
mnemonics.content: T-C-F: Try risky code → Catch specific errors → Finally do cleanup always

mnemonics.title: Catch Block Order
mnemonics.content: CATCH: Children first, Ancestors last - Think specificity!

mnemonics.title: Exception Hierarchy Remember
mnemonics.content: T-RE: Throwable is Root, Exception & Error branch

acronyms.expansion: try code that might fail, catch the failure, handle gracefully
acronyms.usage: Used for handling runtime errors without program termination

acronyms.expansion: Java Virtual Machine - executes bytecode and manages exceptions
acronyms.usage: Throws exceptions when runtime errors occur

acronyms.expansion: Anything that can be thrown/caught in Java
acronyms.usage: Superclass of Exception and Error classes

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a try block as a glass container - if anything inside 'spills' (exception), the catch block acts as a mop to clean it up, and finally is the disinfectant that runs always
Think of Throwable as a family tree: Grandparent Throwable has two children Exception (recoverable) and Error (fatal). Exception has RuntimeException (unchecked) branch and other checked exceptions
Like a key-lock system: Each catch block is a lock, the exception is the key. More specific exceptions have more precise keys. Put specific keys first!

analogies.analogy: Like a safety net under a trapeze artist: Try = performing the act, Catch = safety net catching if they fall, Finally = cleanup crew always present
Like a hot potato: When an exception is thrown, it's passed up the call stack. Each method can catch it or pass it up. If nobody catches it, program crashes
Checked = a required safety protocol you must acknowledge (signature), Unchecked = optional safety gear (you can ignore but it's risky)
